definition: Ability to direct attention toward something.
Understanding that others can direct their attention toward something; also called "sociality."
Understanding that others know you're directing attention toward something, and you know they're directing attention toward something; enables language.
The gesture in the Originary Event that aborted appropriation through temporal delay in representation.
The imperative to defer violent centralizing within social contexts.
Third-order attentionality enabling shared attention and arbitrary language.
Linguistic reconfiguration removing individuals from communal embedment through removal or abstraction.
Oscillation between desire and deferral when something is desired but socially prohibited from appropriation.
One's ability to modulate inherited frames and construct formal objects through signing.
Cultural prejudice favoring executive control over consensus-based decision-making.
Practice of deritualization by exposing preliminary practices of ritualization.
Variability in people's ability to sign, creating differences in agency and hierarchies.
Space where attention is jointly held on a particular object, involving enforced equality and ritual.
Sharing and mutual awareness of a common line of attention on an object; precursor to ethics.
Power to modulate a context by revealing and naming, restoring coherence to relations.
Co-mingling of dread, veneration, and wonder when encountering the originary ostensive.
Conceptual overthrow of divine kingship leading to post-sacrificial exchange.
First person to usurp the center, creating hierarchy to prevent resentment-based destruction.
Ensuring something is done in accordance with a discipline's own rules and guidelines.
Linguistic mismatch where declaratives banish dangling imperatives, undermining ostensive-imperative nexus.
Ability to abstract people, groups, knowledge, and technologies from their embedment for transfer.
Locus of attention and significance where resentments focus and are deferred.
Quality of being centered; degree to which something commands attention.
Order where articulation of power from center through ranks is transparent and consistent.
Occupying the center and attracting attention to direct focus toward another center.
Rejecting the existence of a center, obscuring one's own desires while usurping centrality.
Starting point for issued commands, from which all other commands stem.
Ostensive grounding and justifying imperatives and broader discourse; cannot be formally integrated.
Model involving obedience to deferring violent usurpation of the sovereign center.
Mutual transfiguration between language users' models of reality, creating new shared centers.
Forming and breaking up information into larger or smaller units through differentiation and integration.
Ability to cooperate within cultural reality using community-wide social norms.
Transcendence of simple imitation; ability to construct formal objects and modulate frames.
Culture discouraging unilateral imperative issuance, requiring multiple parties in decision-making.
Linguistic theory contending meaning resides in formulaic constructions, not individual words.
Aesthetic oscillation between attending to signs designating objects and deferred appropriation.
Moment of embarrassment represented to oneself after the fact as social phenomenon.
Point drawing focus within culture; society modeled as concentric circles with divinity central.
Deferral of violence through representation; shared norms converting joint-attentional spaces intentionally.
Speaking about something detached from its physical presence.
Culture where everything must be explained in propositional statements.
Language's main function; substitution for conflict and violence through representation.
Process of substitution of sacrality through scientific atomization.
Appetite for something generated by social prohibition; mimetically influenced by peers.
Space of shared attention turning studied objects into new signs through inquiry.
Belonging in communal and ritual context within local community.
Formal practices particular to disciplinary spaces; exchanging ostensives and converting shared attention.
New inscription that re-inscribes all existing ones.
Culture valuing individual initiative and clear individual leadership responsibility.
Victim of violence by center occupant; activates liberal categories like justice and equality.
Bringing new objects to group attention or leading shared frames through asymmetry in speech.
Linguistically represented concepts within consciousness.
Received/inherited historical configuration instantiated and slightly modified on a scene.
Ability to formally construct objects of shared attention; transcending resentment limitations.
Command derived from ostensive; action warranted by ostensive or explicit command.
Acting in reciprocation with center; society depends on bedrock of reciprocal obligation.
Gap between command issued and command obeyed; minimized through proper commanding.
Always trying to get word from center; nested tacit imperatives exist in all discourse.
Direction of attention to something attended from; elaborates discrepancies between sign and referent.
Creates constructs substituting for originary center; sophisticated reductions, contrasted with wisdom.
Ability to direct intention and knowing others have intention; enables teaching and commanding.
Inquiring into imperative; asking what to do if imperative cannot be fulfilled.
Inquiry into imperatives; instantiates new imperative resisting imperative exchange.
The capacity to cooperate with others toward shared goals, enabling coordination and mutual understanding.
A form of social organization where a single individual occupies the sacred center.
A system of arbitrary signs enabling humans to represent absent objects and defer violence through symbolic communication.
A political-cultural orientation emphasizing individual demands upon central authority rather than seeking commands from it.
The tendency to imitate others' desires and actions, a fundamental social dynamic that can generate conflict or cultural transmission.
The framework governing how significance is shared and deferred within a community.
The sovereign act of identifying and articulating centers and their imperatives.
A cultural stage where representational scenes expand so widely that multiple centers exist simultaneously, enabling individualism.
The initial state where all members of a community possessed equal access to the sign before hierarchies developed.
The hypothetical singular moment when language emerged through deferred appropriation of a central object.
The foundational human scene of representation establishing joint attention and deferral through signing.
A sign indicating something physically present, fundamental to language's origin and ongoing function.
The capacity to modulate frames and redirect collective attention toward new centers.
The negative emotion arising from desire for something socially prohibited or unequally distributed.
Repeated ceremonial actions commemorating the originary scene and maintaining shared attention on centers.
That which commands ultimate attention and significance within a community's shared frame.
A form of governance where the ruler embodies sacred authority and mediates divine will.
The process of projecting collective resentment onto a peripheral figure to restore center.
The spatial and temporal configuration where representation and signification occur.
A representation referring to an object absent from the immediate scene, fundamental to language.
The central authority possessing power to command and interpret centers.
The ritual tearing apart of a victim; related to private imagination's origins.
Relating to victim-centered moral frameworks that judge centers through exemplary suffering.
Physical or symbolic force causing harm, what linguistic representation fundamentally defers.
The capacity to grasp why something is meaningful, transcending layers of sophistication.
